block_name: Themed projects, consolidation and problem solving
unit_title: Themed projects, consolidation and problem solving

content.plain_english: This week in Year 6 maths, your child is working on Themed projects, consolidation and problem solving. They are deepening skills that connect to earlier learning and lay the groundwork for what comes next. Ask them to tell you one thing they learned today — it is the best way to start a maths conversation.
content.in_school: In lessons, children use practical resources and visual representations to explore themed projects, consolidation and problem solving. They work from hands-on activities through to diagrams and then written methods, with class discussion and partner work helping every child understand and apply what they have learned.
content.home_activity: Spend 5 minutes exploring themed projects, consolidation and problem solving together. Ask your child to explain what they have been learning in school — teaching someone else is one of the best ways to understand something deeply. Use everyday objects if that helps, and follow your child's lead.
dinner_table_questions: What does themed projects, consolidation and problem solving mean in your own words?
What was the trickiest part of themed projects, consolidation and problem solving today?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: Look back over what you have learned to check and strengthen understanding.
Use your knowledge in a new or different situation.
Explain why an answer is correct using mathematical language.
Working through a challenge by choosing and using the right maths skills.
A planned method used to approach and solve a problem.

content.times_table_tip: Year 6 children use all their times tables to tackle complex problems including long multiplication, division, and fractions. Challenge them: 'What is 7 × 12?' or 'How many 8s in 72?' Rapid recall frees up thinking space for harder work.
